to have this opportunity to present Brighton early.

chief Executive of the Foundation, joined by my colleague,
uh, Dr. Julie McDonald, who runs our research
and track two, uh, engagement program.
Uh, we are here to share insights on our longitudinal

the first seven minutes, uh, describing how, um,
the work we do, uh, helps broader, uh,
strategic priorities for New Zealand and helps build
and support New Zealand's all important Asia relations.
Um, Julia will then spend about the same amount of time,
and we should have 15 minutes for questions, if that's okay.
Great. Great. Um, so who we are a non-profit,
non-partisan, uh,
expert organization focused on helping New Zealanders
understand and connect to Asia.
Um, we've been doing this work for 30 years,
and we are currently a staff of 24.
Um, we work across government, uh, business
and civil society, um, both here and across Asia.
Um, obviously over the 30 years,
our programs have evolved a lot.
Um, but we currently run eight programs
where we are targeting, um, uh, young New Zealanders
and building, um, a pipeline of Asia capable Kiwis,
uh, both here and across Asia.
Um, we also run the Asia Media Center,
which is New Zealand's largest dedicated Asia news site.
Um, our networks, uh, we have 550 young leaders
and 280 entrepreneurs across New Zealand,
Southeast Asia, and India.
Uh, as you will all know, Asia is home to 60%
of the world's population,
and by 2032 thirds of, uh,
global consumers will be Asian.

and those universities are going, um, you know,
those countries that host those universities are currently
going from net exporter of students that have ambitions
to be importers.
Um, Asia contains nearly 60%
of the world's global Gen Z population.
Uh, this younger generation who are currently in their teens
and twenties is obviously tech savvy shaped by digital media
and whose careers will be profoundly influenced by ai.
Um, at the same time, um, I'm sure this committee knows, uh,
the region is facing tensions, military build ups, um,
unprecedented military expenditure, tariffs
and protectionism, um, major power competition,
shifting alliances and so on.
And part of our job is
to not only help shine a light on these changes

So we support New Zealand's Asia engagement

capable workforce.
We offer internships across Asia,
their business internships, media internships,

to this pipeline

So it's a, uh, for um,
that in itself I think is a bit of a,
for New Zealand and Asia relations. Mm-hmm.
Yeah, absolutely. Alright, thank you very much.
Unfortunately, we have come to end of our time, um,
but really appreciate your insights there
and I think it's a great idea that you're looking
at some more mini surveys too.
Mm-hmm. Obviously we've seen a lot of things happening.
Yeah. Um, you know, from the defense side, Thailand,
Cambodia and India, Pakistan, yeah.
A lot In the last few months as well,
so would potentially impact on that.
But um, yeah, I think we would be very keen
to stay in touch as well.
Great, great. As a committee.
So thank you again for coming in. Thank you.
Thank you. Cheers.
